Title: Ukraine slams Russian 'cynicism' ahead of separate truces
Article:
KYIV: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Tuesday accused Russia of "utter cynicism" for launching deadly attacks while also seeking a truce to stage its May 9 Victory Day parade in Moscow.
Several people were killed in overnight strikes that came after both Moscow and Kyiv announced unilateral ceasefires over different dates this week.
Moscow has announced a ceasefire during public World War II celebrations over May 9, and Kyiv has said it will halt fire over May 6.
"It is utter cynicism to ask for a ceasefire in order to hold propaganda celebrations while carrying out such missile and drone strikes every single day leading up to it," Zelenskyy said in a statement in response to the attacks.
"Russia could cease fire at any moment, and this would stop the war and our responses," he added.
The Russian May 9 parade is typically a bombastic display of military strength, which since 2022 has sought to link Soviet victory over Nazi Germany with the invasion of Ukraine.
But the Kremlin ordered a scaled-back version this year – with no military hardware to be on display – over the fear it could be targeted by Ukraine.
Kyiv has intensified its retaliatory long-range strikes in recent weeks, hitting a spate of Russian oil facilities and a luxury high-rise building in Moscow.
It calls the strikes fair retaliation for Russia's nightly bombarding of its cities with drones and missiles.
Overnight, a Russian strike killed four people in the central Poltava region – employees of state energy firm Naftogaz and first responders, triggering outrage in Kyiv.
"Two of the killed were first responders, killed in a vile double-tap strike targeting those who arrived to help people at the scene of the attack.
"Only a terrorist state like Russia employs inhuman and criminal tactics like these," Ukraine's Foreign Minister Andriy Sybiga said.
One person was also killed in the northeastern Kharkiv region as Russia fired 11 ballistic missiles and 164 drones across the country, according to authorities.
Russia's defence ministry said it had downed more than 300 Ukrainian drones between late Monday and early Tuesday.
Despite the order of temporary halts to fighting, there is no sign that the four-year war is close to being resolved at the negotiating table.
Short-term ceasefires are not infrequent, with the two sides having suspended long-range attacks over Orthodox Easter last month.
Moscow is demanding Kyiv fully withdraw its troops from the eastern Donbas area and renounce Western military support – ultimatums seen as tantamount to capitulation in Kyiv, which has rejected them.
On the battlefield, Russia's progress has stalled – with its army losing more territory than it captured in April for the first time since summer 2023, according to an AFP analysis of data from the Institute for the Study of War (ISW).
The war has spiralled into the bloodiest conflict in Europe since World War II, claiming the lives of hundreds of thousands of people and displacing millions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Affin Bank, Sime Darby rev up premium banking push with Porsche campaign
Article:
AFFIN Bank Bhd has partnered with Sime Darby Auto Performance, the Porsche distributor in Malaysia, to launch the "Race to the Extraordinary" campaign as part of its push to strengthen its premium and private banking proposition.
The collaboration marks a first for the banking group in motorsport-led lifestyle engagement.
It will be anchored by access to the Porsche Carrera Cup Asia and the return of the Porsche Esports Sprint Challenge Malaysia.
Affin group president and group chief executive officer Datuk Wan Razly Abdullah said the partnership reflects the bank's ambition to deliver differentiated client experiences beyond traditional banking services.
"We are delighted to elevate our partnership with Porsche Malaysia, bringing together two brands that value performance, precision and innovation," he said at the campaign launch today.
Wan Razly said the initiative aligns with the Affin Axelerate 2028 (AX28) plan which focuses on customer service, digital leadership and responsible banking.
Sime Darby Auto Performance CEO Christopher Hunter said the partnership connects aspiration with opportunity, particularly for individuals pursuing their ambitions.
Under the campaign, Affin clients will gain exclusive access to race viewing experiences at the Sepang International Circuit on Aug 22-23 and the Marina Bay Street Circuit from Oct 9-11 this year.
The collaboration also highlights Malaysian driver Naquib Azlan, who represents Team Porsche Malaysia in the 2026 Porsche Carrera Cup Asia season, as part of efforts to inspire younger audiences and motorsport enthusiasts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Media organisations encouraged to utilise RM30mil innovation fund - Teo
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Media organisations are encouraged to apply for the RM30 million Media Innovation Fund, which is now entering its fifth phase, Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said.
She said applications for the latest phase are open from May 1 to 14, with each organisation eligible to apply for up to RM300,000 covering four key areas: workforce training, software and technology adoption, equipment, and strengthening support facilities and content.
To date, 34 media organisations have been approved for funding totalling RM12.38 million under the first phase, while applications for phases two to four are still under review.
"So, I urge media organisations that have yet to apply to seize this opportunity. This is already the fifth phase, and if the fund is fully utilised, there may not be a sixth or seventh phase this year," she told reporters after the closing ceremony of a course on artificial intelligence (AI), online safety and media reporting ethics, here today.
Also present were Tun Abdul Razak Broadcasting and Information Institute (IPPTAR) director Datuk Roslan Ariffin and head of Academic Division Mohammad Yusari Ameer Mohd Yusof, as well as Malaysian Media Council board member Teh Athira Yusof.
Elaborating, Teo said the fund was introduced to support the media industry amid mounting challenges driven by shifts in the advertising landscape.
"We hope this fund can assist our media industry, especially as advertising revenues are increasingly captured by social media platforms," she said.
In a related development, she stressed the importance of digital literacy and fact-checking practices in an AI-driven environment, amid the growing spread of misinformation.
As an example, she highlighted AIFA (Artificial Intelligence Fact Check Assistant), a chatbot developed by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission and accessible via WhatsApp in multiple languages.
"While AIFA may not answer every question, what's important is that when we see or receive information, we should not rush to share it," she said, adding that a culture of verification must be nurtured.
Since its launch in 2025, she said, AIFA has received an average of 434 queries daily, although more promotion is needed to boost usage.
In her speech earlier, Teo said the Malaysian Media Council has implemented Phase Two of its complaints mechanism from April 8, requiring all complaints to be submitted via its official platform instead of email.
She said the approach was to ensure that complaints management was more structured, transparent and professional, thus increasing public confidence.
"As of April 30, the council had received 10 complaints involving reporting accuracy, unauthorised use of images and the impact of content on social harmony. Of the total, five cases have been resolved, while the remainder are under review," she added.
The two-day course, organised by IPPTAR in collaboration with the council, involved 27 media practitioners across platforms and focused on AI, online safety and ethical reporting in the face of evolving technological challenges.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Israel court extends detention of two Gaza flotilla activists to Sunday: rights group
Article:
ASHKELON, Israel: An Israeli court has extended the detention of two foreign activists from a Gaza-bound flotilla until Sunday, a rights group representing them said, as authorities continue to question the pair.
Spanish national Saif Abu Keshek and Brazilian Thiago Avila appeared before a court in the city of Ashkelon for their second hearing on Tuesday, after they were brought to Israel for questioning last week.
"The court approved their detention until Sunday morning," Miriam Azem, international advocacy coordinator at the Israeli rights group Adalah told AFP.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fuel station attendant fined RM10,000 for misusing MyKads to buy subsidised petrol
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Bangladeshi petrol pump attendant was fined RM10,000 by the magistrate's court today after pleading guilty to misusing two MyKads to purchase the subsidised Budi Madani Ron95 petrol last month.
Magistrate Aina Azahra Ariifin also ordered Md Jakir Hossain, 51, to serve seven months in jail for each of the two charges if he fails to pay the fine.
He was charged with two counts of possessing and misusing the MyKad of two individuals, aged 25 and 31, without valid authority or legitimate reason to hold another person's identification card.
The offences were committed between February 8 and April 22, and between April 21 and 22, at a petrol station along Jalan Pahang.
The charges were framed under Rule 25(1)(o) under the National Registration Regulations, which carries a maximum RM20,000 fine or a jail term up to three years or both upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Hench Goh urged the court to impose a proportionate sentence as a deterrent.
"The court must consider the losses incurred as the victims lost their subsidised petrol quota.
"The accused could have returned the MyKad to the victims, but instead chose to use them to purchase petrol, despite knowing they did not belong to him," he said.
Jakir's lawyer, Low Carmen, appealed for a lenient sentence, and said he is the sole breadwinner for his family.
"He was arrested on April 23 and detained for 12 days before he was brought to court today.
"He is remorseful and has learnt his lesson," he said.
Investigations revealed that one victim, an accountant, noticed her subsidised petrol had dropped from 94.937 litres to 44.637 litres, leading her to suspect misuse of her MyKad.
After contacting the Budi Madani Contact Centre, she was informed that five suspicious transactions had been made at a petrol station using her MyKad, which she had lost and believed had been taken by a staff member.
The suspect was later arrested at his rented room, where police seized two MyKads after reviewing the petrol station's closed-circuit television footage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia closes higher on late buying
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia reversed earlier losses to end higher on Tuesday as buying interest gained momentum in the afternoon session, said an analyst.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 7.66 points, or 0.44 per cent, to close at 1,747.43 from Monday's close of 1,739.77.
The benchmark index, which opened 1.97 points lower at 1,737.80, moved between a low of 1,732.64 and a high of 1,747.82 throughout the day.
Market breadth was negative with losers leading gainers 605 to 539. A total of 599 counters were unchanged, 889 untraded, and 11 suspended.
Turnover decreased to 2.86 billion units worth RM2.59 billion compared with 3.05 billion units worth RM2.55 billion yesterday.
More to come
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mara weighs listing of two education arms
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) is in the early stages of evaluating a potential listing of two of its higher education institutions.
A structured decision framework is expected within next year with a possible timeline extending to 2028, Mara director-general Zulfikri Osman said.
Zulfikri said the review involves Universiti Kuala Lumpur (UniKL) and Universiti Poly-Tech Malaysia (UPTM), which he described as Mara's strongest-performing education entities.
"We have already started engaging with a couple of advisory firms, and planning is underway," he said, adding that discussions remain preliminary.
"Maybe after one year, I will be able to provide a more accurate update," he told reporters at a special press conference on Mara's investment sector here today.
Zulfikri said both institutions are currently profitable and, based on initial assessments, appear to meet financial requirements typically associated with public listings.
He added that Mara is working towards a potential listing within three years, with 2028 viewed as a tentative target, although no firm timeline has been set.
Key parameters, including listing structure and timing, remain under review.
He also said it is still too early to determine whether the listings would be on Bursa Malaysia's Main Market or ACE Market, as Mara continues to assess the most appropriate route with its advisers.
Early internal evaluations suggest both institutions could potentially qualify for the main market, but the final decision will depend on advisory input and regulatory considerations.
Zulfikri noted that listings of education institutions in Malaysia remain rare, particularly among universities, making the exercise relatively unconventional in the local capital market.
Separately, UPTM declared a RM19.97 million dividend for the financial year ended 2025 to Mara via Mara Corporation Sdn Bhd, marking its third consecutive year of stable payouts.
Between 2023 and 2025, UPTM recorded steady growth in revenue and profit, supported by higher student enrolment and improved cost efficiency.
Performance was further strengthened by expanded industry-linked programmes and strategic partnerships, which enhanced graduate employability and reinforced the institution's position in the education sector.
The higher dividend was also driven by diversification of income streams, including growth in professional and executive education as well as wider adoption of digital and hybrid learning models.
UPTM chief executive officer Radzuan Razali said the sustained performance reflects the effectiveness of the group's medium-term strategy implemented over recent years.
He said the dividend also underscores UPTM's continued role in supporting Mara's financial sustainability, aligned with its mandate in national education and human capital development.
"The increase in dividends reflects not only current performance, but also a stronger underlying business foundation," he said.
"We have restructured our operating model with a focus on industry-based programmes, cost efficiency and expansion into premium education segments."
Looking ahead to 2026, Radzuan said UPTM is targeting double-digit revenue growth and improved profit margins, driven by expansion in strategic campuses including Cyberjaya and Sarawak, as well as growth in executive education and flexible learning offerings.
Over the next three to five years, he said the group aims to strengthen its position as a leading provider of professional education in the region.
He added that UPTM will continue enhancing industry partnerships to ensure programmes remain aligned with evolving job market needs, in line with the national agenda for Bumiputera human capital development.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Landlords beware: Don't pay for your tenant's bitcoin mining—transfer your TNB account
Article:
KUANTAN: Property owners are advised to transfer Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) accounts into their tenants' names when renting out properties.
This move is aimed at preventing landlords from shouldering the burden of potential hefty electricity bills if their premises are used for illegal activities, including bitcoin mining.
Deputy Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad said the change of account holder would protect landlords in cases where tenants make illegal electricity connections.
"When landlords rent out their premises, some are unaware of their tenants' activities. It is therefore best to transfer the electricity account to the tenant's name.
"If tenants make illegal electricity connections for bitcoin mining, resulting in high monthly bills, landlords will be spared from liability and authorities will instead take action against the tenants," he told reporters at Taman Mahkota Aman here today.
Earlier, Rahman, together with officers from the Energy Commission and TNB, raided two houses during a special operation dubbed Op Steal, as part of a crackdown on unauthorised bitcoin mining using stolen electricity.
Rahman said that in most electricity theft cases, authorities identify the property owner through utility bills, Indah Water Konsortium records, and quit rent documents.
"If the tenant has fled and the TNB account remains under the landlord's name, then the landlord will have to bear the consequences," he said.
Meanwhile, Rahman said inspections at most premises found that electricity meters had been bypassed to power bitcoin mining operations, with cables that did not comply with safety standards set by the Energy Commission.
"The wiring is often in disarray, and the use of numerous high-powered mining machines operating simultaneously can cause short circuits or even explosions. This poses a fire risk to the respective property and nearby homes.
"We urge the public to remain vigilant and report suspicious activities at vacant properties. Premises used for illegal bitcoin mining are often monitored only through closed-circuit television systems, without any human presence," he said.
It was reported last year, more than 30 property owners in Kuala Lumpur were
facing millions of ringgit in electricity claims
after their tenants allegedly engaged in illegal bitcoin mining activities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM directs air force to hasten cloud seeding ops in northern states
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has ordered the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) to expedite cloud seeding operations in northern Peninsular Malaysia following the prolonged dry spell.
Anwar said the government has also instructed the Fire and Rescue Department to continue providing assistance in the ongoing operations over the next few days.
"We are aware that the northern region, particularly Kedah, is experiencing a longer-than-usual dry season, which has caused difficulties for farmers and padi growers.
"Therefore, I have instructed the Air Force to expedite cloud seeding operations, and have also asked the Fire and Rescue Department, which has more limited capacity, to continue its efforts over the next few days," he said at a press conference on the global supply crisis.
Yesterday, Anwar also instructed the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) to speed up cloud seeding operations in states currently at critical levels, particularly Kedah and Perlis.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/05/1432307/pm-instructs-metmalaysia-expedite-cloud-seeding-kedah-perlis?source=widget
He said the move is urgently needed to address current weather conditions, and suggested the use of drone technology to overcome logistical constraints and ensure smooth implementation.
"I have instructed MetMalaysia to expedite its implementation, including exploring the use of drone technology and cooperation with various parties to address the shortage of suitable aircraft, to ensure the well-being of the people is protected," he said in a post on his official Facebook page.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bertam Valley residents awarded RM4.7mil in damages over 2013 floods
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: One hundred residents of Bertam Valley, Cameron Highlands, were awarded RM4.7 million in damages over the 2013 floods, which led to loss of lives and property damage.
Lawyer M. Manogaran, representing the residents, said the quantum of damages was assessed today by judge Datuk Ahmad Shahrir Mohd Salleh at the Seremban High Court.
The residents had succeeded in their negligence suit against Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) on May 25, 2018.
Then High Court judge Datuk Nordin Hassan found that TNB, as the owner and operator of the Sultan Abu Bakar Dam (ESAB), had acted negligently, causing the flood that resulted in loss of lives and property damage.
Manogaran said the court ordered TNB to pay RM912,459.76 in special damages to the plaintiffs.
He said the court also awarded RM30,000 in general damages to each plaintiff, and exemplary damages of RM10,000 to 83 plaintiffs.
"The total damages awarded amount to RM4,742,459.76. The High Court also ordered interest at a rate of five per cent per annum on the sum, calculated from May 25, 2018, until full settlement.
"The court took into account the trial court's finding that TNB was fully liable and negligent in the manner the Sultan Abu Bakar Dam in Ringlet, Cameron Highlands, was operated and maintained," he said.
Manogaran said general damages were awarded in consideration of the pain, suffering, trauma and distress experienced by the plaintiffs.
He said exemplary damages were imposed as punishment for the defendant's failure to properly maintain the dam despite having the capability to do so.
Lawyer Datuk Alvin John appeared for TNB.
In his judgment, Nordin said the release of water from the dam on Oct 23, 2013, was not a proper decision, as the dam was not equipped with functioning flow measurement instruments to determine water flow and the appropriate volume to be released.
He said that in the absence of such instruments, it could not be concluded that the three water releases on the day of the incident were appropriate, as the defendant had no clear knowledge of the volume of water that should have been discharged.
Nordin, now a Federal Court judge, said it was undisputed that no flow measurement instruments had been installed at ESAB prior to the incident, and that such instruments were only installed after the flood.
In those circumstances, the court found that TNB had failed to maintain appropriate water levels at the dam, particularly during the monsoon season.
He said the failure to install such instruments constituted negligence, as it placed the lives and property of residents at risk – a risk that ultimately materialised in the flood.
On Dec 11, 2018, TNB failed in its appeal at the Court of Appeal to set aside the High Court's decision.
The Federal Court dismissed TNB's application for leave to appeal on Nov 12, 2019, rendering the High Court's ruling final.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspect in fatal Ketereh college student stabbing to be charged soon  - Kelantan cops
Article:
KOTA BARU: A suspect in the fatal stabbing of a college student in Ketereh last Friday, in which the victim was stabbed 61 times, is expected to be charged soon.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said investigations into the case are about 70 per cent complete.
"We are in the process of completing the investigation papers and only one suspect will be charged," he said when contacted.
Another suspect who was previously remanded will be released on police bail.
"The suspect is expected to be charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder," he added.
In the 12.30am incident on Friday, the body of a woman was found in Kampung Padang Simah, Ketereh, with 61 stab wounds across her body.
Yusoff said the victim was found without any identification documents, but police managed to identify her through fingerprint records with the National Registration Department.
He said the victim is believed to have been killed elsewhere before her body was dumped at the location.
Following the incident, police
remanded four individuals, including two elderly persons
who are the parents of one of the suspects. The elderly couple were released on police bail yesterday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Spy in a Cheongsam' Blossom Wong laid to rest
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former police officer and undercover operative Blossom Wong Kooi Fong, famously known as the "Spy in a Cheongsam", was laid to rest today in a solemn and intimate ceremony attended by family members and close friends.
Wong, 88, who retired in 1993 with the rank of superintendent, was given a quiet final send-off following a lifetime of service in the police force.
Her funeral rites began with a two-day wake at Nirvana Memorial Park in Bukit Seputeh before the hearse departed at 11am for Nirvana Memorial Park in Shah Alam, where the cremation ceremony was held at noon.
At the crematorium, her daughter, Dr Christina Blossom Welch, led family members in placing flowers on Wong's coffin as a final gesture of farewell.
She was later handed Wong's framed photograph as her remains were wheeled in for the cremation process.
The service was conducted in a solemn and quiet atmosphere, reflecting the close-knit nature of the gathering.
Those closest to Wong remembered her as a motherly, kind and fiercely dedicated woman who balanced a demanding career with deep devotion to family and colleagues.
Former colleague Angie Eng, 73, who worked with her at the Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID), described Wong as a principled officer who never compromised on procedure.
"She never took shortcuts. She was very by-the-book, but at the same time, she was loving, sweet and always cared for her staff," she said.
Her niece, Wong Wei Kim, described her as warm and welcoming, adding that Wong always made time for loved ones despite her busy schedule.
"She was very kind to all of us, always welcoming and hospitable. No matter how busy she was, she made sure we were comfortable and well fed," she said today.
She added that Wong's chicken curry was a favourite among her nieces and nephews, while her homemade chocolate cake was a much-loved dessert among family members.
Wong was also known for her discipline and composure, both at work and in life, and was a keen squash player.
At the same time, she remained deeply committed to her family.
"She was fiercely family-driven. Not many people can balance both work and family the way she did, but she always made time," Wong Wei Kim said.
At the crematorium, former high school mate Olive Sze said she only learnt of
Wong's passing
after reading about it in the New Straits Times.
"After reading the NST, that was when I knew it was her. From there, I came to the funeral based on the information I saw," she said, adding that she immediately recognised Wong from the photograph published.
She also described Wong as an "icon" and likened her to "James Bond" during their school days.
Wong died at Kuala Lumpur Hospital on May 1, 2026, at the age of 87, after suffering a stroke.
Wong leaves behind a legacy of service, discipline and compassion, remembered not only for her role in law enforcement but also for her warmth and unwavering dedication to those around her.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kedah police bust 'Geng Atan' loan shark syndicate, solve 11 cases
Article:
ALOR STAR: Police have detained five suspects, including a married couple, believed to be members of the "Geng Atan" syndicate, for criminal intimidation in Sungai Petani.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said police received a report on April 16 from a 27-year-old man who claimed his vehicle was set on fire after he failed to repay an online loan.
"A series of raids and arrests conducted from April 26 led to the arrest of four men and one woman, aged between 31 and 43. Initial screenings showed all suspects tested positive for drugs and had prior criminal records," he told a press conference here today.
He said further investigations revealed the suspects acted as hired executors to deliver warning notes, splash paint, and burn victims' vehicles.
"They received instructions from a mastermind who is still at large. They were paid between RM300 and RM600, depending on the criminal act committed," he said.
Adzli said the group, which has been active since February, also recruited other individuals, including drug addicts, to carry out these activities.
The arrests have enabled police to solve 11 reported cases.
Police are tracking down two more suspects, and the case is being investigated under Section 5(2) of the Moneylenders Act 1951, Sections 435 and 427 of the Penal Code, as well as Sections 12(2) and 15(1) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952.
"I advise the public not to borrow from loan sharks. In this case, the victim applied for a RM200 loan but received only RM150. Even though he had fully repaid the loan, the suspects continued to harass him," Adzli said.
In an unrelated case, police arrested a 45-year-old mechanic and seized two pistols, two air rifles, an imitation pistol, live ammunition, and steel pellets during a raid at a vehicle repair premises in Baling on May 1.
"Preliminary investigations found the suspect purchased the firearms from a friend in a neighbouring country, while the air rifles were self-assembled. The suspect claimed all firearms were for his personal collection," he said.
He added that the suspect tested positive for methamphetamine and has been remanded until Thursday for investigations under Section 7 of the Firearms Act 1971, Section 8(a) and Section 36 of the Arms Act 1960.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fraud defence, SME financing top Public Bank's priorities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Public Bank Bhd says it will prioritise fraud prevention, digitalisation and small and medium enterprise (SME) financing in 2026.
The bank will also seek to sustain growth through cross-selling and non-interest income expansion over the next three to five years.
Managing director and chief executive officer Tan Sri Dr Tay Ah Lek said Public Bank remains cautious of global headwinds but expects domestic resilience to support its core businesses.
"The group will continue pursuing synergistic growth strategies in consumer banking and commercial lending, with a focus on home, SME and hire purchase financing," he said at the bank's 60th annual general meeting today.
Tay added that the bank is targeting sustained double-digit return on equity supported by its leading market position and disciplined cost management.
To complement lending growth, the bank will further expand non-interest income, particularly in wealth management, capital markets and transactional services.
"Tapping on our large customer base, there are strong opportunities for cross-selling across the group and its subsidiaries," said Tay.
Meanwhile, digitalisation remains central to this strategy with continued investments in customer analytics, digital platforms and new product offerings.
The bank has enhanced its MyPB Web and MyPB App platforms, contributing to a 52.3 per cent surge in mobile banking transactions in 2025.
Tay said the group is also leveraging artificial intelligence in fraud detection, enabling real-time monitoring of customer activity and early identification of suspicious transactions.
As of March 2026, its fraud prevention measures protected over 1.6 million customers and prevented about RM2 billion in potential losses.
On SMEs, Tay reiterated that the segment remains a core pillar of the bank's strategy.
"SME lending has always been our priority. We have a very resilient SME portfolio because we have supported SMEs from day one.
"We are very passionate about ensuring SMEs have access to financing, and we will sustain our leading market share in this segment," added Tay.
He said the bank continues to provide working capital, factory financing and tailored packages to meet diverse business needs.
The bank recorded 10.6 per cent growth in domestic SME financing in 2025, reflecting continued expansion opportunities.
The group is also engaging SMEs affected by slower business conditions and global uncertainties.
"We are reaching out preemptively. If they need additional working capital or restructuring, we will provide flexible repayment assistance.
"As long as customers are still striving to stay afloat, we will support them," he added.
On asset quality, Tay said the bank remains cautious but confident of its current position.
"Overall asset quality remains stable at this moment, but we will monitor closely and assist customers if conditions worsen," he said.
He added that the bank remains disciplined in balancing growth with prudent lending practices to preserve asset quality.
Public Bank's pre-tax profit rose 6.8 per cent to RM9.54 billion, while net profit increased 1.1 per cent to RM7.22 billion for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2025,
Loan growth expanded 5.1 per cent to RM445.76 billion, while deposits rose 3.2 per cent to RM447.11 billion.
The bank maintained strong fundamentals, including a net return on equity of 12.8 per cent and cost-to-income ratio of 34.9 per cent.
Public Bank declared a dividend of 22.5 sen per share, with a payout ratio of 60.5 per cent, and aims to sustain similar levels going forward.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police submit investigation paper on Melor assemblyman to DPP
Article:
KOTA BARU: The investigation paper concerning allegations that Melor assemblyman Wan Rohimi Wan Daud belittled police officers during a roadblock has been submitted to the deputy public prosecutor.
Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the paper was submitted last week for further action.
"At the police investigation level, it is complete as it has been submitted to the deputy public prosecutor.
"The decision on whether to charge or otherwise rests with the deputy public prosecutor," he said when contacted.
It was previously reported that Wan Rohimi had given his statement at the Kota Baru district police headquarters over the allegation.
The statement was recorded after Kelantan police opened an investigation into an individual who made
statements belittling the security forces
regarding a viral video of a motorcycle convoy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: KLIA Aerotrain expected to resume 24-hour operations by end of May
Article:
SEPANG: The Aerotrain service at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) is expected to resume full 24-hour operations by the end of May.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the restoration process is now in its final phase after Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) completed all necessary technical tests.
He explained that a full report on the tests is being submitted to the Land Public Transport Agency (Apad) for evaluation and safety validation before full operations commence.
"It is expected that after validation, the Aerotrain will operate 24 hours a day by the end of this month.
"At present, the service is still running, but we have had to halt operations for a few hours at night to allow for maintenance and track inspection work.
"By the end of this month, the entire service will be fully restored to run 24 hours," he told reporters after launching the MyKLIA and MyPutrajaya Monthly Travel Card (MTC) campaign here today.
The Aerotrain service at KLIA drew serious attention after the rail system reportedly suffered frequent technical failures over the past few years due to the 25-year age of the assets.
The worst breakdown occurred in March last year when the service had to be completely suspended following a mechanical failure that forced passengers to walk along the tracks to the satellite building.
Following the incident, MAHB took measures by starting a new train set replacement programme and upgrading the rail infrastructure to ensure optimum safety and operational efficiency.
Throughout the replacement period, shuttle bus services were provided intensively to manage passenger movement between the main terminal and the satellite building.
The restoration of 24-hour operations is seen as a crucial step in restoring KLIA's image as a major international aviation hub, while reducing reliance on the shuttle bus services used previously.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China fireworks factory explosion kills 26, injures 61
Article:
BEIJING: The death toll from a giant explosion at a fireworks factory in central China rose to 26, with 61 more injured, officials said Tuesday.
The explosion occurred at around 4.43pm on Monday at the Liuyang Huasheng Fireworks Manufacturing and Display Company in Liuyang, Hunan province, state broadcaster CCTV reported.
Following the blast, all fireworks makers in Hunan's provincial capital Changsha, which administers Liuyang, had been ordered to stop production ahead of safety inspections, CCTV said.
Videos on social media from Monday showed continuous explosions accompanied by a vast cloud of smoke rising high into the air in a rural area surrounded by mountains.
Drone footage from CCTV taken a day later showed a swathe of smouldering debris where buildings had stood, with rescue workers and excavators scouring the rubble.
Smoke continued to rise from some buildings left standing, many of them with their roofs blown off.
Changsha mayor Chen Bozhang told a news conference on Tuesday afternoon that another five people had died since earlier reports that 21 were killed.
"We feel deeply grieved and filled with remorse," Chen said, adding that search and rescue work was "basically complete."
[[nid:1432804]]
The central government had sent experts to guide rescue efforts, while more than 480 rescuers had been urgently dispatched to the site, according to CCTV.
They had established a 3-kilometre (1.9-mile) control zone around the site and evacuated people nearby.
Police had apprehended the company's management while investigations into the cause of the accident continue, CCTV said.
President Xi Jinping had called for "all-out efforts" to treat the injured, search for missing persons, and for those responsible to be held accountable, state news agency Xinhua reported.
Liuyang is a major fireworks hub, producing around 60 percent of the fireworks sold in China and 70 percent of those exported.
Industrial accidents, including in the fireworks industry, are common in China due to lax safety standards.
Last year, an explosion at another fireworks factory in Hunan killed nine people, and in 2023, three people were killed after blasts struck residential buildings in the northern city of Tianjin.
In February, separate explosions at fireworks shops in Hubei and Jiangsu provinces killed 12 and eight people.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Myanmar man charged with ex-wife's murder
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Myanmar man was charged at the magistrate's court with the murder of his ex-wife two weeks ago.
Aung Tun Ngwa, 26, nodded to indicate he understood the charge read out to him in Bahasa Malaysia before magistrate Izzah Hasan Basri.
No plea was recorded as murder cases are under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
He is accused of murdering 33-year-old Merry Yet Ngwa, also from Myanmar, at 2.30pm in front of a condominium block at Taman Setapak Indah Jaya in Wangsa Maju on April 23.
The charge framed under Section 302 of the Penal Code carries the death penalty or a jail term of between 30 years and 40 years and no less than 12 strokes of the rotan if the death penalty is not imposed.
Aung did not have legal representation.
The prosecution was represented by Inspector M. Naresh Kumar.
The court fixed July 31 for mention and submission of a post mortem report.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AI expected to contribute up to RM20bil annually to Malaysia's GDP by 2030 –Gobind Singh
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Artificial intelligence (AI) is expected to contribute between RM13 billion and RM20 billion annually to Malaysia's gross domestic product (GDP) by 2030, said Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo.
He said AI is already demonstrating tangible economic value in Malaysia, with projections indicating it could add between 0.8 and 1.2 percentage points to Malaysia's GDP growth each year.
"At the national level, the digital economy, increasingly powered by AI, contributed over 23 per cent to Malaysia's GDP last year, amounting to more than RM450 billion in economic value.
"These are not projections in isolation. They signal a structural shift where AI is no longer peripheral to the economy, but central to productivity, competitiveness and national growth," he said in his speech at the 5th International Conference on Youth 2026 (ICYouth) hosted by Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM) here, today.
His speech text was read by Deputy Digital Minister Datuk Wilson Ugak Kumbong.
In his speech, Gobind also said the ministry will soon launch the Government Innovation Initiative (GII), a platform that opens up real problem statements from the public sector for innovators, researchers and youth to develop solutions using emerging technologies, including AI.
"GII is not just about innovation; it is about guided innovation. It ensures that experimentation happens within a framework, where ideas can be tested, refined, and scaled responsibly.
"In this context, institutions like UPM are critical. They provide the technical capability, research environment and talent base that can directly respond to the problem statements surfaced through the GII," he noted.
Gobind also stressed that the long-term impact of AI depends on how it is developed and used, especially by the younger generation.
"Ultimately, the future of AI will not be determined by technology alone, but by the values we embed within it. The systems designed, the models trained and the data used by today's youth will influence real decisions and real lives," he said, adding that platforms like ICYouth are important as they bring together researchers, policymakers, industry players and youth to ensure innovation is balanced with responsibility.
The conference, organised by UPM Institute for Social Science Studies (IPSAS), carries the theme 'AI and Youth for Societal Impact: Transforming Communities and Advancing Wellbeing', focusing on how AI technology can be used in an inclusive and ethical way to improve social wellbeing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor says JS-SEZ, RTS growth will not drive up cost of living
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Johor government has assured that rapid economic expansion under the Johor–Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) and Johor Baru–Singapore Rapid Transit System (RTS) will not come at the expense of rising living costs.
State Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Committee chairman Lee Ting Han said while both mega projects are expected to generate strong economic spillover, the state is mindful of potential pressure on food prices, rental rates and housing costs.
"We do not deny that JS-SEZ and RTS will bring significant economic gains to Johor. But unmanaged growth can place pressure on the cost of living, especially food, rent and housing," he said at the State Legislative Assembly sitting at Bangunan Sultan Ismail here today.
He was responding to Marina Ibrahim (DAP-Skudai), who asked what measures are in place to prevent rapid development from driving up the cost of living amid concerns over rising prices of food, rentals and basic goods in Johor Baru.
Lee said the state's approach is to ensure growth remains inclusive, backed by targeted interventions that directly benefit daily life.
He said the expanded Bantuan Kasih Johor (BKJ) programme has channelled more than RM506 million across four phases to hundreds of thousands of recipients, including senior citizens, low-income households and vulnerable groups.
"In the latest Phase Four,
we are allocating RM138 million
covering 22 new categories of recipients. This provides immediate relief amid current global economic pressures," he said.
However, Lee said cash aid alone is not a long-term solution.
He pointed to structural measures, particularly in the food sector, where rising prices are often driven by operational costs such as rental, raw materials, logistics and labour.
As part of this, Johor has introduced a pilot initiative at Medan Selera Kampung Melayu Majidee, offering lower rental rates to traders while residents receive Kasih Johor vouchers to help sustain purchasing power.
"This creates a balanced ecosystem – traders can maintain reasonable prices, while the public continues to enjoy affordable meals," he said.
On enforcement, Lee said the state is working closely with the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN), including price monitoring, festive price controls and action against profiteering.
He said housing remains a key pressure point, with initiatives such as RM200 monthly rental assistance for eligible low-income groups and an increase in first-time homebuyer aid to RM2,000.
More than 20,000 units of Rumah Mampu Milik Johor
have been completed between 2022 and 2025, with another 10,000 units expected this year as part of a long-term target of 100,000 units by 2030.
To ease daily expenses, Lee said the state is also expanding the free Bas Muafakat Johor service alongside federal public transport initiatives.
"Johor's strategy is holistic. We are not only giving direct aid, but also addressing structural costs and building long-term solutions," he said, adding that the goal is to ensure development lifts living standards rather than burdening the rakyat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PILA set to anchor Penang as regional logistics hub
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow described the Penang International Logistics Aeropark (PILA) as a game changer for the state's logistics and industrial landscape.
During a visit to the project site, Chow said the strategic joint venture between the Penang Development Corporation (PDC) and Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) would anchor Penang's position as a regional logistics hub.
Chow, who is also PDC chairman, said PILA would feature a new cargo terminal designed to strengthen the northern region's logistics ecosystem while supporting the continued expansion of Penang's industrial base.
"PILA is a critical leap in future-proofing Penang's logistics infrastructure, especially for the semiconductor and electrical and electronics (E&E) sectors.
"It will boost cargo capacity and open the door for both local logistics players and global companies to scale up their operations here," he said.
Chow said that despite global headwinds, Penang's industrial sector — particularly E&E and semiconductors — remains resilient, underpinned by sustained demand for emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI).
"This has translated into strong and growing order books, reinforcing the urgency to expand our logistics capabilities," he added.
Chow said without additional facilities, cargo from the northern region would continue to be diverted to other hubs, including Singapore, for export.
"With PILA, we can retain and uplift our cargo throughput locally.
As MAHB aptly puts it: 'Make in Penang, fly from Penang'," he said.
Earlier, Chow was briefed by PDC special projects unit chief Azizee Rohemee and Arup project manager Jason Mak.
Also present were MAHB senior manager Rahime @ Faize Jamaluddin, PDC chief executive Abdul Latiff Ab Aziz, PDC technical division manager Norhartini Mohd Noor and PDC corporate and communications head Zulkifli Ismail.
The boards of PDC and MAHB have approved the formation of a joint venture company, PILA Sdn Bhd, with the agreement signed on April 13, 2026.
The project is funded by PDC with federal government support through targeted grants.
The initiative aligns with Penang Vision 2030 and the State Structure Plan 2030, complementing the expansion of Penang International Airport while addressing future logistics demands of the high-tech sector.
The project has secured master plan approval, with earthworks and soil treatment now underway.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/04/1417391/penang-gets-planning-approval-logistics-aeropark-masterplan
Its first phase — the development of a Free Commercial Zone air cargo warehouse — is slated for completion in 2029.
Once fully realised, PILA is expected to handle up to 500,000 tonnes of cargo annually by 2050, with warehouse space exceeding two million sq ft.
The first phase alone will add capacity for an additional 100,000 tonnes per year.
The RM1 billion development, spanning 42 hectares, has already attracted leasing commitments from both international logistics firms and local operators.
Chow said a formal launch is in the pipeline and is expected to be officiated by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Family seeks court order for murder probe into Teoh Beng Hock's death
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The family of Teoh Beng Hock has filed a fresh legal bid to compel police to investigate his death 17 years ago as murder.
Beng Hock's brother Teoh Meng Kee and his parents Teoh Leong Hwee and Teoh Shuw Hoi filed the leave application to initiate a judicial review via Messrs Lim Wei Jiet last month.
The applicants named the Inspector-General of Police, the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director, the police, the Attorney-General (A-G), and the government as respondents.
They seek a declaration compelling the respondents to investigate under Section 302 of the Penal Code for murder and Section 304 for culpable homicide not amounting to murder.
They are also seeking an order to compel the respondents to carry out investigations under both provisions within 120 days from the date of the court order.
Lawyer Lim Wei Jiet confirmed the matter when contacted, adding that the High Court has fixed May 18 to hear the leave application.
In a supporting affidavit, Meng Kee claimed the respondents had failed to provide any reasons or explanation as to why no investigation had been initiated under Sections 302 and 304 of the Penal Code.
He claimed the case remained a long-standing matter of public interest, yet no individual or officer from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) had been charged, despite a Court of Appeal ruling on the case.
Meng Kee claimed in a letter dated May 20, 2025, Selangor police CID informed that investigations were conducted under Section 342 of the Penal Code for wrongful confinement.
He claimed the CID also said that the Attorney-General's Chambers had decided on No Further Action (NFA) in the case.
He claimed that in a subsequent letter dated May 30, 2025, the A-G wrote to the family's former solicitors to explain the basis for the NFA decision.
"I am advised by my lawyers and believe that, in essence, the police have conveyed to us that no investigation has been initiated or completed under the offences of murder and culpable homicide.
"If this judicial review application is not heard, it will result in the family being unable to pursue justice over the deceased's death," he claimed.
On Nov 21, 2024, the High Court ordered the police to complete investigations into Beng Hock's death within six months after allowing a judicial review application by his parents.
Beng Hock, who was the political secretary to then Seri Kembangan assemblyman Ean Yong, was found dead on July 16, 2009, at Plaza Masalam in Shah Alam after giving a statement at the MACC office on the 14th floor.
On Jan 5, 2011, the Shah Alam Coroner's Court ruled that his death was neither suicide nor homicide and found no third-party involvement.
However, on July 21, 2011, the Royal Commission of Inquiry concluded that his death was due to suicide.
On Sept 5, 2014, the Court of Appeal overturned the open verdict and held that his death resulted from unlawful acts by one or more unidentified persons.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Immigration 'game changer': New NIISe system to clear travellers in seconds at KLIA
Article:
SEPANG: Kuala Lumpur International Airport Terminal 1 is set for faster passenger processing with a new integrated digital clearance system capable of clearing travellers in four to five seconds.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the system, known as the National Integrated Immigration System (NIISe), will be implemented in phases and is expected to significantly ease congestion at both arrival and departure points.
"This is significant. It will help us avoid congestion at airports, whether during arrivals or departures. It is truly a game changer," he told reporters after a working visit to the terminal today.
Also present were Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban, Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain and Home Ministry secretary general Datuk Awang Alik Jeman.
The system allows travellers to clear immigration using three methods: facial recognition, QR codes or passwords across 65 automated gates.
Despite the push for efficiency, Saifuddin stressed that security will not be compromised.
"Our principle is consistent, smooth movement cannot override security. We want efficient movement of people and goods, but there can be no compromise on security," he said.
During the visit, Saifuddin was briefed on end-to-end screening processes, including how luggage is handled from entry into the system to inspection, sorting and enforcement action.
"I saw how luggage is processed, screened and sorted, including how prohibited items such as drugs or weapons are detected.
"This gave me a clearer understanding of how the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency carries out its responsibilities," he said.
He added that the visit gave him a clearer picture of the agency's operational capabilities and ongoing improvements.
Among measures already introduced are dedicated lanes for "Nothing to Declare" and "Goods to Declare", aimed at speeding up passenger flow while ensuring compliance with regulations.
Saifuddin also highlighted the use of risk profiling and intelligence-led enforcement, where inspections are focused on high-risk individuals rather than blanket checks.
"This approach allows us to maintain efficiency without compromising enforcement effectiveness," he said.
He, however, acknowledged that minor disruptions may occur during the transition to the new system.
"There will be a transition phase, and with any system replacement, there may be technical glitches. We will do our best to minimise them, but they are part of the process.
"Even the most advanced systems may face minor disruptions during transition, and we will mitigate them," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Energy crisis fuels calls to cut methane emissions
Article:
PARIS: Officials pushed Monday for faster action to reduce methane emissions from the fossil fuel sector to both help slow climate change and boost energy security as the Middle East war chokes off supply.
Using its role as rotating chair of the Group of Seven (G7) industrialised powers, France convened government officials, industry leaders and experts to build momentum on cutting methane emissions ahead of the UN's COP31 climate summit in November.
Methane, the second-biggest contributor to climate change, stays in the atmosphere for far less time than carbon dioxide, but its warming effect is roughly 80 times more potent over a 20-year period.
French Ecological Transition Minister Monique Barbut said she hoped the Paris meeting would help to "accelerate the implementation of effective solutions to reduce methane emissions."
"Of course, action on methane is not a fight of any single actor, and nobody can win it alone," Barbut said.
Barbut said the world remains "very far" from meeting a global pledge to cut methane emissions by 30 per cent by 2030 compared with 2020 levels.
Around 60 per cent of methane emissions are linked to human actions.
The fossil fuel sector – oil, gas and coal – accounts for 35 per cent of methane emissions from human activity, the International Energy Agency said in a report.
"Yet there is still no sign that methane emissions from fossil fuel operations are falling, despite well-known and proven mitigation pathways," according to the IEA's Global Methane Tracker 2026.
Such emissions from the sector – which come from leaks, flaring and venting from oil and gas operations – remained "near record highs" in 2025, the report said.
Officials at the Paris conference said cutting leaks and flaring from the fossil fuel industry could increase the availability of energy while slashing planet-heating emissions.
The energy crisis "certainly gives everyone another reason why they should act with alacrity", the prime minister of Barbados, Mia Mottley, told AFP.
"The question is, can some of this (gas) be brought on stream in the short to medium term, certainly quicker than it would take to repair some of the production facilities that have been lost in the Middle East as a result of the war," she added.
The European Union's energy commissioner, Dan Jorgensen, also stressed that more gas could be put on the market if leaks were prevented.
"This shows that methane abatement and energy security are not competing priorities," Jorgensen told the conference.
"Methane is the single fastest lever we have to limit near-term warming. We can no longer wait to pull this lever," he added.
Oil prices have soared since the United States and Israel launched the war against Iran in late February and Tehran closed the Strait of Hormuz in response.
The IEA said 20 per cent, or around 110 billion cubic metres, of the world's liquefied natural gas (LNG) flowed through the Strait of Hormuz last year.
Nearly 100 billion cubic metres of natural gas could be made available annually through a global effort to cut methane from oil and gas operations, the IEA said.
A further 100 billion cubic metres would be unlocked through the elimination of non-emergency flaring worldwide, it added.
"Reducing methane emissions remains one of the best things we can do to slow global warming while cleaning up our air, improving public health, and increasing our energy security," British energy minister Ed Miliband said in a video message.
France will continue pushing the issue for COP31 with "means of action" and "slightly new strategies", a French ecological transition ministry official said on condition of anonymity, without providing details.
Agriculture is also a major emitter through the methane released by livestock during digestion as well as rice cultivation, where flooded fields create ideal conditions for methane-emitting bacteria.
Landfills also emit methane. The UN Environment Programme announced that it was expanding its satellite-based global methane detection system to track emissions from coal mines and waste facilities for the first time.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM700 million lost annually to illegal bitcoin mining power theft
Article:
KUANTAN: Electricity theft linked to illegal bitcoin mining activities costs Malaysia an estimated RM700 million annually.
Deputy Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad said the rampant illegal activities nationwide remain a major concern, with authorities stepping up enforcement against offenders.
"Today, we raided two houses in Kuantan for stealing electricity for illegal bitcoin mining.
"The first consumed about 40,703 kilowatt-hours daily, with losses estimated at RM21,000 a month, while the second, with 10 machines, recorded losses of about RM13,700 a month.
"The country faces about RM700 million in losses annually due to illegal electricity theft by crypto miners. Imagine the impact over 10 years if we do not carry out integrated enforcement operations and put a stop to the theft," he said to reporters at Perkampungan Seri Mahkota Permai here today.
Earlier, Abdul Rahman, together with officers from the Energy Commission and Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB), raided the two houses during a special operation dubbed Op Steal, as part of a crackdown on unauthorised bitcoin mining activities using stolen electricity.
Abdul Rahman said efforts to combat electricity theft would be intensified nationwide to reduce losses, with authorities deploying modern methods to detect such activities.
"The Energy Commission and TNB not only have officers with the expertise to detect electricity theft, but are also using modern technology, including drones, to track down illegal cryptocurrency mining operations.
"Op Steal will be conducted regularly, not only in Pahang but also expanded to other states. I will be on the ground during these operations," he said.
Meanwhile, Abdul Rahman said the enforcement team inspected the two houses and cut off their electricity supply, as they were believed to be involved in illegal bitcoin mining, with estimated losses reaching RM34,000 a month. No arrests were made.
He said both cases would be investigated under Section 37(3) of the Electricity Supply Act 1990, which provides for a fine of up to RM50,000, imprisonment of up to one year, or both, upon conviction.
Repeat offenders may face fines of up to RM100,000 or imprisonment of up to three years, or both.
Also present were Energy Commission compliance and enforcement division director Ismail Zaili Yusop, deputy chief executive officer (technical regulation and compliance office) Roslee Esman, and TNB chief distribution network officer Ahmad Sazree Abd Aziz.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Govt to expand Jualan Rahmah Madani to 30,000 locations nationwide
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government will expand the Jualan Rahmah Madani programme to 30,000 locations nationwide this year as part of efforts to reduce the cost of living.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the programme will be implemented in all state constituencies and conducted on a weekly basis to ensure consistent access for consumers.
He added that the initiative, which offers goods at 5 to 30 per cent below market prices, will also be expanded to selected rural and village areas.
"Last year, the government implemented more than 25,000 Jualan Rahmah programmes involving all state constituencies in the country. So I want this to be implemented immediately, and for this year, we have announced 30,000 Jualan Rahmah programmes.
"This means an expansion. First, to all state constituencies, and second, it must be carried out once every week. In this way, consumers will have certainty on where goods are available, as well as fixed days and schedules.
"We also want to ensure that the Jualan Rahmah programme is held not only in urban fringe areas, but also in selected rural and village areas," he said at a press conference on the global supply crisis.
Anwar said efforts will also be made to ensure small traders benefit from the programme.
"In implementing this programme and ensuring it benefits small traders, we want to make sure that small traders are also involved.
"We do not want this to become a platform dominated by large companies, but rather to ensure that the benefits of the Jualan Rahmah Madani programme allow small traders to sell their goods at lower prices.
"Of course, this initiative does not solve all problems, but I believe it can help ease the current burden. God willing, through our weekly engagements and Cabinet meetings every Wednesday, we will continue to examine and explore new initiatives to reduce the burden borne by the people."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Govt-linked agencies to cut commercial rents to ease pressure on small traders
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Rental rates for commercial premises owned by government-linked agencies will be reduced to help ease the financial burden on small traders and businesses.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said agencies such as Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) and Uda Holdings Bhd will begin implementing the reductions this month, with details to be announced at the district level.
"Last week, before Friday prayers, I visited a food stall in Rawang. The operator, although polite and composed, shared her concerns about having to cope with rising costs of goods, even if the increases were small.
"But small increases every week, over several weeks, will eventually have a significant impact and make it difficult for her business.
"In response to the concerns, I have sought ways to ease their burden within our means. I believe the first step we must take is to reduce rental rates.
"Most of these premises are owned by government-linked companies or agencies such as Mara and Uda. Starting this month, rental rates at all their business premises will be reduced, with announcements to be made at the district level soon," he said at a press conference on the global supply crisis.
Anwar, who is also the finance minister, called on state governments and local authorities to follow suit, noting that several, including Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) and the Selangor government, have already taken similar steps.
He said the move is aimed at easing operational pressures faced by small traders, hawkers and food operators amid rising costs.
"Following the example of DBKL, which has already reduced rental rates, I call on all local authorities and state governments to do the same starting this month.
"I would also like to thank Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari for responding positively and announcing that rental rates for business premises — especially those occupied by small traders, hawkers and food operators — will be reduced soon.
"While this falls under the jurisdiction of local authorities and state governments, I urge them to implement these reductions immediately and ensure they take effect this month to help ease the burden faced by traders and hawkers."
In March, DBKL announced a
50 per cent reduction
in rental rates for hawker sites and selected premises under its supervision, effective from April 1 until Dec 31, 2027.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the initiative is expected to benefit more than 10,000 hawker sites and small business premises owned by DBKL in the federal capital.
She said the move aims to help reduce operating cost pressures faced by hawkers and micro-traders, particularly amid rising living costs.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Govt moves to arm border personnel in high-risk areas
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is moving towards arming personnel of the Malaysia Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA), particularly those deployed in high-risk areas.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said a policy decision has been reached to equip the agency with weapons, with implementation to be overseen by the ministry's top leadership.
"At the policy level, we have agreed that MCBA will be equipped with weaponry. This will be managed by the ministry's top leadership in the near future," he told reporters after a working visit to KLIA Terminal 1 today.
Also present were Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban, MCBA director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain and Home Ministry secretary-general Datuk Awang Alik Jeman.
Saifuddin said discussions are ongoing on the types of weapons to be introduced, including the possibility of starting with non-lethal options.
"There are views that we should begin with tasers, as border situations do not always require firearms. We are studying this carefully," he said.
However, he stressed that the policy direction is clear.
"The bottom line is that MCBA will move towards the use of weapons," he said.
Saifuddin noted that some personnel, particularly those with prior service in the police, already have the necessary firearms training.
"If a commander comes from the police, they already possess the required weapons training. We are essentially equipping them accordingly," he said.
He emphasised that border enforcement is inherently high-risk and requires personnel to be adequately prepared.
"This is not a low-risk environment. Border control involves various threats. Our personnel are trained, both psychologically and in terms of skills, to handle these challenges," he said.
It was previously reported that MCBA is expected to be equipped with firearms by year-end. Saifuddin had said several options were under consideration, including tasers and light firearms such as pistols.
On Feb 25, an assassination attempt was made on Bukit Kayu Hitam commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Mohd Nasaruddin M. Nasir while he was driving to perform subuh prayers at about 6.30am.
Two gunmen on a motorcycle opened fire, hitting his vehicle. He was not injured.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Padi farmers to get RM200 advance payment to ease operating costs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government has agreed to provide a RM200 advance payment under the padi cultivation incentive scheme to help ease cash flow pressures faced by padi farmers.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the move, approved at the National Economic Action Council meeting, is aimed at supporting padi farmers who are affected by rising operational costs, including land preparation and machinery rental.
The initiative involves an allocation of RM48 million and is expected to benefit about 240,000 registered padi farmers nationwide.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Home Ministry to expand Malaysian Border Guard intake this year
Article:
SEPANG: The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) will expand its manpower under the Malaysian Border Guard (MBG) initiative this year.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the agency has been given approval to recruit up to 200 border guards.
He said the first intake of 50 personnel has already been completed, with deployments underway at key entry points including Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) and Port Klang.
"At KLIA alone, 18 personnel from the first intake have already been placed.
The second intake is currently in progress," he told reporters after holding a working visit at the KLIA Terminal 1 today.
Also present were Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban, MCBA director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain and Home Ministry secretary general Datuk Awang Alik Jeman.
Saifuddin said the recruits come from diverse professional backgrounds, including individuals with commando training, drill instructors and other specialised skills.
"We want to assess their performance first. I do not foresee any issues, and if this approach proves effective, we are open to expanding recruitment further," he said.
He added that the Home Ministry would continue supporting the initiative if it
delivers value
to MCBA operations.
Last month, the government launched the MBG initiative under the MCBA, marking a key step in strengthening security and management at the country's entry points.
The first batch of 48 veterans, who underwent psychometric, physical and interview assessments, will be deployed immediately after the ceremony.
The initiative, unveiled at the Malaysian Prison Department Academy, aimed to enhance border control capabilities while addressing manpower shortages at critical checkpoints.
The programme involves recruiting retired soldiers, with an initial intake of 200 veterans approved under the RM8 million allocation announced in the 2026 Budget.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AI expected to contribute up to RM20b yearly to Malaysia’s GDP by 2030, says Gobind
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 5 — Artificial intelligence (AI) is expected to contribute between RM13 billion and RM20 billion annually to Malaysia’s gross domestic product (GDP) by 2030, said Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo.
He said AI is already demonstrating tangible economic value in Malaysia, with projections indicating it could add between 0.8 and 1.2 percentage points to Malaysia’s GDP growth each year.
“At the national level, the digital economy, increasingly powered by AI, contributed over 23 per cent to Malaysia’s GDP last year, amounting to more than RM450 billion in economic value.
“These are not projections in isolation. They signal a structural shift where AI is no longer peripheral to the economy, but central to productivity, competitiveness and national growth,” he said in his speech at the 5th International Conference on Youth 2026 (ICYouth) hosted by Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM) here, today.
His speech text was read by Deputy Digital Minister Datuk Wilson Ugak Kumbong.
In his speech, Gobind also said the ministry will soon launch the Government Innovation Initiative (GII), a platform that opens up real problem statements from the public sector for innovators, researchers and youth to develop solutions using emerging technologies, including AI.
“GII is not just about innovation; it is about guided innovation. It ensures that experimentation happens within a framework, where ideas can be tested, refined, and scaled responsibly.
“In this context, institutions like UPM are critical. They provide the technical capability, research environment and talent base that can directly respond to the problem statements surfaced through the GII,” he noted.
Gobind also stressed that the long-term impact of AI depends on how it is developed and used, especially by the younger generation.
“Ultimately, the future of AI will not be determined by technology alone, but by the values we embed within it. The systems designed, the models trained and the data used by today’s youth will influence real decisions and real lives,” he said, adding that platforms like ICYouth are important as they bring together researchers, policymakers, industry players and youth to ensure innovation is balanced with responsibility.
The conference, organised by UPM Institute for Social Science Studies (IPSAS), carries the theme “AI and Youth for Societal Impact: Transforming Communities and Advancing Wellbeing”, focusing on how AI technology can be used in an inclusive and ethical way to improve social wellbeing. — Bernama

Title: Scorching streaks: MetMalaysia warns Kelantan, Pahang and Sabah hit by Level One heatwave
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a Level One (Yellow) heatwave status for four areas in Peninsular Malaysia and one in Sabah as of 4pm today.
MetMalaysia said the four areas are Jeli dan Kuala Krai in Kelantan, as well as Jerantut and Maran in Pahang, while the area in Sabah involves the district of Telupid.
The Level One heatwave status is issued when an area records a maximum daily temperature of 35 to 37 degrees Celsius for at least three consecutive days.
For the latest information on weather in Malaysia, visit the official MetMalaysia website at
https://www.met.gov.my/iklim/status-cuaca-panas
. — Bernama

Title: Two statutory body teaching staff remanded in Terengganu over alleged RM30,000 bribery case, says MACC
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU, May 5 — Two employees of a statutory body in Terengganu have been remanded for allegedly soliciting and accepting bribes of around RM30,000 in return for issuing competency certificates, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Agency said today.
An MACC source said both male suspects, in their 20s and 40s, who worked as teaching staff at the statutory body, were remanded for four days till May 8 today following their arrest yesterday when they showed up to provide their statements at the state MACC office.
“Preliminary investigations revealed that both suspects allegedly colluded to commit the offence in 2025 and the bribes solicited were for issuing ‘immediate approval’ of competency certificates for every participant of the course.
“Both suspects were believed to have received between RM900 and RM1,500 for each of the 31 participants, which was paid through fund transfers into their personal bank accounts,” they said.
The source also related that the commission was examining if the certification fraud occurred on a larger scale and that it was possible that every certificate issued by the related agency could be reviewed to ensure its authenticity.
Terengganu MACC director Hazrul Shazreen Abd Yazid confirmed the arrests and remand, adding that the case was being investigated under Section 16(a) of the MACC Act 2009.
He also did not reject the possibility of other arrests being made to facilitate investigations. — Bernama

Title: Malaysia studies taser use as first step to arming Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency, says home minister
Article:
SEPANG, May 5 — The Home Ministry is studying a proposal to use electric stun guns (tasers) as an initial step in efforts to equip personnel of the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) with weaponry.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said a policy decision has been agreed upon to provide weaponry for AKPS, with implementation to be carried out in the near future.
“There are views suggesting that we begin with the use of taser guns, as situations at the border may not always require the use of firearms. This matter will be examined,” he said at a press conference after inspecting operations at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1 here today.
He did stress that the government’s direction is clear towards equipping AKPS personnel with weaponry to strengthen border control capabilities, pointing out that border control duties involve high risks and require personnel who are psychologically prepared and well-trained.
He said personnel with backgrounds in security forces such as the police already possess weapons-handling skills and only need to be equipped with the necessary tools.
In addition, Saifuddin Nasution said AKPS has been allowed to recruit up to 200 Malaysian Armed Forces veterans this year under the Malaysian Border Guard (MBG) initiative to strengthen control at the country’s entry points, with the first phase of recruitment involving 50 personnel completed and the remainder to follow in subsequent phases.
MBG personnel have been deployed at several main entry points, including KLIA and Port Klang, he said, adding that they possessed diverse skill sets, including commando training, drill instructors and other expertise that can support border control operations.
“Here at KLIA Terminal 1 alone, there are 18 personnel who have just completed training. The second intake is currently underway and is expected to be completed this year,” he said, adding that his ministry was open to proposals from AKPS Director-General Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain if the recruitment approach proves to add value to the organisation. — Bernama

Title: Myanmar man charged with murder of ex-wife in Setapak condominium stabbing case
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 5 — A Myanmar national was charged at the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of his ex-wife in front of a condominium block in Setapak last month.
Aung Tun Ngwa, 26, who is unemployed, nodded in understanding after the charge was read out before Magistrate Nurul Izzah Hasan Basri.
However, no plea was recorded from the accused as murder cases fall under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
Aung was charged with murdering Merry Yet Ngwa, 33, also a Myanmar national, in front of Block 1 of Genting Court Condominium, Taman Setapak Indah Jaya, near here, at 2.30pm on April 23.
The charge was framed under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which provides for the death penalty or imprisonment of not less than 30 years and not more than 40 years, along with a minimum of 12 strokes of the cane, upon conviction.
The court fixed July 31 for mention of the case.
Prosecuting officer Insp M. Naresh Kumar appeared for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.
